---
title: Backups & playit
description: Declare what can be archived and how tunnels should work.
---

Two optional blocks round out a production-quality template: backup targets and playit tunnel
support.

## Backup targets

The `backups` object declares which container paths users can archive:

```json
"backups": {
  "enabled": true,
  "targets": [
    {
      "id": "world",
      "label": "World",
      "containerPath": "/data/world"
    },
    {
      "id": "full",
      "label": "Everything",
      "containerPath": "/data"
    }
  ]
}
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    enabled: {
      description:
        "Whether backups are offered for instances of this template.",
      type: "boolean",
      required: true,
    },
    targets: {
      description: "Paths that can be archived. At least one when enabled.",
      type: "BackupTarget[]",
      required: true,
    },
  }}
/>

Each target needs an `id` (stable identifier), a human `label`, and an absolute `containerPath`.
Users pick targets by label when backing up manually or defining
[backup policies](/docs/guides/backups).

**Design tip:** offer one focused target for the irreplaceable data (the world) and one broad
target for full recovery (the whole data dir). Focused targets make frequent scheduled backups
cheap; broad ones make disaster recovery simple.

## Playit tunnels

The `settings.playit` object declares tunnel support:

```json
"settings": {
  "playit": {
    "enabled": true,
    "protocol": "tcp",
    "port": 25565
  }
}
```

<TypeTable
  type={{
    enabled: {
      description: "Whether instances of this template offer the Playit tab.",
      type: "boolean",
      required: true,
    },
    port: {
      description: "The container port players connect to.",
      type: "number",
      required: true,
    },
    protocol: {
      description: "tcp, udp or both. Match what the game actually speaks.",
      type: "'tcp' | 'udp' | 'both'",
    },
  }}
/>

Pick the protocol carefully: Minecraft Java is `tcp`, Bedrock is `udp`, some games need `both`.
With this declared, users get one-toggle public addresses; see the
[playit guide](/docs/guides/playit) for the user-facing flow.
